Kalatkat | Sayao, Sibonga | 2026 GPX"},"content":{"rendered":"\n

Mt. Kalatkat is a short out-and-back hike in Kalangyawon, Sayao, Sibonga. The route follows grassy mountain slopes and narrow livestock paths to the summit, with gradual elevation gain, open mountain views, and a straightforward trail suitable for first-time solo hikers.<\/p>\n\n\n

Trail Snapshot<\/h3>
EASY<\/div>View others \u2192<\/a><\/div>
\n
\ud83d\udcc5 Documented<\/span>May 2026<\/strong><\/div>
\ud83d\udccf Distance<\/span>3.5 km (Out and Back)<\/strong><\/div>
\u26f0 Elevation Gain<\/span>184 m<\/strong><\/div>
\ud83d\udd52 Duration<\/span>1-2 hrs<\/strong><\/div>
\ud83d\udca7 Water<\/span>At least 1 liter<\/strong><\/div>
\ud83c\udf33 Shade<\/span>Limited<\/strong><\/div>\n
\ud83d\udc63 Surface<\/span>30% trail, 70% road<\/strong><\/div>
\ud83d\uddfa\ufe0f GPX<\/span>Supporter Access<\/a><\/strong><\/div> <\/div><\/div>\n\n\n\n

Access<\/h2>\n\n\n\n

The documented hike began near Kalangyawon Elementary School, which was reached via a combination of paved and concrete roads. Motorcycle parking was available along the roadside, and no parking fee was observed during the hike. Private vehicles could access the jump-off point, while public transportation was not available. Habal-habal services were available.<\/p>\n\n\n\n

Motorcycles can reach the foot of the mountain, reducing the hiking distance. For the documented hike, the route began from Kalangyawon Elementary School to include a longer warm-up walk.<\/p>\n\n\n

Why this trail is rated Easy<\/h3>
\u2713 Easy-to-follow trail<\/div>
\u2713 Communities located along the route<\/div>
\u2713 No dangerous cliff exposure<\/div>
\u2713 Suitable for first-time solo hikers<\/div>
\u2713 Short hiking distance<\/div><\/div>

On this trail:<\/span> cliff<\/a> \u2022<\/span> peak<\/a><\/p><\/div><\/div><\/div>\n\n\n\n

Route Description<\/h2>\n\n\n\n

The trail gradually climbs through grassy mountain slopes and narrow livestock paths toward the summit. The route is easy to follow and provides wide, open views along much of the hike.<\/p>\n\n\n\n

Most of the trail has gradual elevation gain with little technical difficulty. Near the summit, the trail becomes more exposed before returning along the same route to the starting point.<\/p>\n\n\n\n\n\n\n

Trail Conditions<\/h2>\n\n\n\n

Conditions during the documented hike were sunny and dry. The lower sections were mostly shaded, while the summit area was more exposed. No stores were encountered along the route.<\/p>\n\n\n\n

Mobile signal was intermittent throughout the hike. At least 1 liter of water was recommended. Very few people and few insects were encountered during the hike.<\/p>\n\n\n\n

Additional Notes<\/h2>\n\n\n\n

The trail is well-trodden because it is regularly used by local residents to move livestock.<\/p>\n\n\n\n
